title: Delicate German Semolina Pudding
description: A light German dessert featuring semolina, with a delicate mustard flavor and a glassy-jelly texture, made in a ramekin.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, combine the milk, sugar, and salt, and heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally.
In a small bowl, whisk together the semolina, mustard, and a pinch of salt.
Gradually whisk the dry ingredients into the milk mixture, and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Remove the saucepan from the heat, and stir in the brine, vanilla extract, and cold water.
Pour the mixture into 6 ramekins, and place them in a large baking dish.
Add hot water to the baking dish to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly.
Remove the ramekins from the water bath, and let them cool to room temperature.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until chilled and set.
